steps.title: Factor to read off the roots
steps.content:
Shortcut (Vieta's): Factor out $(x - k)$:
$(x-k)^2 - (k-6a)(x-k) = (x-k)\bigl[(x-k)-(k-6a)\bigr] = (x-k)(x-2k+6a) = 0$
The two roots are $x = k$ and $x = 2k - 6a$.

steps.title: Use sum of roots = 3k + 27
steps.content:
Sum of roots: $k + (2k - 6a) = 3k - 6a$. Set equal to the given sum $3k + 27$:
$3k - 6a = 3k + 27 \Rightarrow -6a = 27 \Rightarrow a = -\dfrac{9}{2}$
Equivalent decimal: $-4.5$. The answer is -9/2.
